subject: This is what days-old beef actually looks like
preheader: 100% grass-fed. Named farms. No hormones, no feedlots. Collected fresh from Brookvale.
body:
We talk a lot about where our food comes from. Today we want to talk about the food itself.

Start with the beef. It comes from Rachel's Farm in Kempsey. 100% grass-fed cattle raised on regenerative pasture. No hormones. No feedlot finishing. When you open the pack, the colour is deep red, not grey. That is what fresh looks like.

The salmon is wild-caught Alaskan, supplied by Collins Wild Caught on the South Coast. It is firm, vibrant, and nothing like the pale farmed fillets you see at the supermarket.

The pork comes from Bundarra Farm. The dairy is biodynamic, from Paris Creek. The eggs are from Farmer Brown.

Every one of these products traces to a farm you can look up. That is the difference between a supplier code and a name.

Supermarket beef can travel thousands of kilometres and sit in cold storage for weeks before you see it. Ours goes from the farm to our Brookvale hub within days. You collect it fresh, Monday to Friday.

No lock-in. No minimum order. Just real food from real farms, honestly priced.

Rachel & the FarmThru team
creative_brief: Product-focused email. Hero image: close-up of 100% grass-fed steak, natural light on timber. Below: product grid showing salmon, pork, dairy, eggs alongside farm names. Single CTA to shop. Let the food sell itself.
